content: In non-cargo based projects, calls to cargo check are disabled, because it doesn't exist. Is there any alternative command in the Makefile that compiles only the rust code? given that the rust/bindings have been generated.
No (at the moment at least) -- what do you need it for?
To not build the whole kernel every time to see if the rustc will give me any errors, after changing a single file.
A build after modifying a file should not rebuild the whole kernel.
If it does, then it sounds like you are cleaning your build directory or similar.
(We may have a bug etc. too, of course, but the point is that it should not be happening that your whole kernel gets rebuilt)
having a target to just check the rust code (so just see if the compiler would emit any typing-errors) would be very useful to me
should be not so hard to support that, I can have a look into that today
would be extremely great if it didn't have to compile any (or almost no) C code, I could then use it in my rust-compile tests I do for pin-init
Yeah that's what I am planning to do

Almost there:

~/devspace/onur-ozkan/linux $ time make LLVM=1 -j $(nproc) rustcheck
  RUSTC CHECK rust/macros/lib.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/pin-init/internal/src/lib.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/build_error.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/ffi.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/bindings/lib.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/uapi/lib.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/pin-init/src/lib.rs
  RUSTC CHECK rust/kernel/lib.rs
make LLVM=1 -j $(nproc) rustcheck  5.04s user 1.16s system 169% cpu 3.654 total
~/devspace/onur-ozkan/linux $

In practice, it does the job of "checking the rust sources without actually building anything and touching any C code" but there are some issues I still need to fix. First, current change in the rust/Makefile is extremely verbose, I need to rework that part. Second, it doesn't handle bindgen, so bindgen files need to be created before running the check (which means it doesn't work on a fresh checkout), I will work on that too. I am off for today (going to watch Eurobasket :) ). I will work on it over the weekend or early next week.

Onur Özkan said:

should be not so hard to support that, I can have a look into that today

I was quite wrong there xd
Is it fast?
Oh, you have numbers right there.
Can you compare it to make rust/ ?

Yeah it is fast compare to make LLVM=1 -j $(nproc) rust/:

make LLVM=1 -j $(nproc) rust/  28.07s user 4.07s system 226% cpu 14.217 total
It's also not only about being faster, with check-only builds you can easily check for cross targets
fwiw my cpu is Intel Core Ultra 7 165U @ 14x 4.9GHz
Very cool!!!
Very cool. It looks like it is actually fast enough to run async on file save.
@Matthew Maurer here + https://lore.kernel.org/r/20250915115311.3025-1-work@onurozkan.dev
The patched was acked by Nicolas here, any idea when it can be landed? It would be very useful for me when I am working on a laptop with limited resources.
As I said in v1, I am not planning to land it, sorry.

Miguel Ojeda said:

As I said in v1, I am not planning to land it, sorry.

Is it due to complexity (I can understand that, it's kind a duplicating a lot of things)? I would be happy to make it cleaner and simpler if that's the case. It would be really useful for me when I am working remotely on a low resource laptop (and I also see that there are other devs interested on this).
The build system is being reworked by Miguel and new feature would complicate that
I see. I guess I will wait that and then implement check build on that
